speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Is Banquo gone from court?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Ay, madam, but returns again tonight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Say to the King I would attend his leisure

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: For a few words.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Madam, I will.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Naught’s had, all’s spent,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Where our desire is got without content.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: ’Tis safer to be that which we destroy

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Than by destruction dwell in doubtful joy.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: How now, my lord, why do you keep alone,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Of sorriest fancies your companions making,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Using those thoughts which should indeed have died

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: With them they think on? Things without all remedy

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Should be without regard. What’s done is done.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We have scorched the snake, not killed it.

speaker: MACBETH
text: She’ll close and be herself whilst our poor malice

speaker: MACBETH
text: Remains in danger of her former tooth.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But let the frame of things disjoint, both the worlds suffer,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ere we will eat our meal in fear, and sleep

speaker: MACBETH
text: In the affliction of these terrible dreams

speaker: MACBETH
text: That shake us nightly. Better be with the dead,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whom we, to gain our peace, have sent to peace,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Than on the torture of the mind to lie

speaker: MACBETH
text: In restless ecstasy. Duncan is in his grave.

speaker: MACBETH
text: After life’s fitful fever he sleeps well.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Treason has done his worst; nor steel nor poison,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Malice domestic, foreign levy, nothing

speaker: MACBETH
text: Can touch him further.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Come on, gentle my lord,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Sleek o’er your rugged looks. Be bright and jovial

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Among your guests tonight.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So shall I, love,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And so I pray be you. Let your remembrance

speaker: MACBETH
text: Apply to Banquo; present him eminence

speaker: MACBETH
text: Both with eye and tongue: unsafe the while that we

speaker: MACBETH
text: Must lave our honors in these flattering streams

speaker: MACBETH
text: And make our faces vizards to our hearts,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Disguising what they are.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You must leave this.

speaker: MACBETH
text: O, full of scorpions is my mind, dear wife!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou know’st that Banquo and his Fleance lives.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: But in them nature’s copy’s not eterne.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There’s comfort yet; they are assailable.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Then be thou jocund. Ere the bat hath flown

speaker: MACBETH
text: His cloistered flight, ere to black Hecate’s summons

speaker: MACBETH
text: The shard-born beetle with his drowsy hums

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hath rung night’s yawning peal, there shall be done

speaker: MACBETH
text: A deed of dreadful note.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What’s to be done?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Be innocent of the knowledge, dearest chuck,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till thou applaud the deed.—Come, seeling night,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Scarf up the tender eye of pitiful day

speaker: MACBETH
text: And with thy bloody and invisible hand

speaker: MACBETH
text: Cancel and tear to pieces that great bond

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which keeps me pale. Light thickens, and the crow

speaker: MACBETH
text: Makes wing to th’ rooky wood.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Good things of day begin to droop and drowse,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whiles night’s black agents to their preys do rouse.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou marvel’st at my words, but hold thee still.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Things bad begun make strong themselves by ill.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So prithee go with me.
